tests.summary: Orders: exactly 10 orders shows single page, no pagination controls
tests.precondition: Logged in as tanweer@test.com; exactly 10 orders exist in history, newest-first, on /orders.

steps.action: Check for page-number controls and Previous/Next.
steps.expectedResult: No pagination controls render (PROPOSED — not yet implemented — [data-test=pagination-prev], [data-test=pagination-next], [data-test=pagination-page-{n}] are absent per AC3).

tests.summary: Orders: 11 orders shows exactly 10 on page 1, newest-first
tests.precondition: 11 orders exist for tanweer@test.com, newest-first, on /orders.

steps.action: Read [data-test=order-id] on the first and 10th row.
steps.expectedResult: First row = newest order (#11); 10th row = the 2nd-newest order.

tests.summary: Orders: Next control (11 orders) shows the 1 remaining order on page 2
tests.precondition: 11 orders exist, currently on page 1 of /orders.

steps.expectedResult: Page 2 renders exactly 1 [data-test=order-row] — the oldest order, continuing newest-first (AC2).

tests.summary: Orders: Previous control is disabled/hidden on page 1
tests.precondition: 11 orders exist, currently on page 1 of /orders.

steps.expectedResult: Control is absent or rendered disabled (AC5).

tests.summary: Orders: Next control is disabled/hidden on the last page
tests.precondition: 11 orders exist; navigated to page 2 (the last page) of /orders.

steps.expectedResult: Control is absent or rendered disabled (AC5).

tests.summary: Orders: direct page-number navigation with 21 orders
tests.precondition: 21 orders exist (3 pages of 10/10/1), currently on page 1 of /orders.

steps.action: Click [data-test=pagination-page-3] (PROPOSED — not yet implemented) directly from page 1.
steps.expectedResult: Page 3 renders immediately, showing the 1 oldest order, without requiring sequential Next clicks (AC4).

tests.summary: Orders: count display shows correct range on page 2 of 23 orders
tests.precondition: 23 orders exist for tanweer@test.com; navigate to page 2 of /orders.

steps.expectedResult: Displays "Showing 11–20 of 23" exactly (AC6's own worked example).

tests.summary: Orders: zero orders shows unchanged empty state, no pagination controls
tests.precondition: Logged in as tanweer@test.com; no orders have been placed.

steps.expectedResult: Existing empty-state message "You haven't placed any orders yet." renders exactly as today (verified live in pages/OrdersPage.tsx); no [data-test=order-count] or pagination controls render (AC7).

tests.summary: Orders: pagination and total count persist across logout/login
tests.precondition: 23 orders exist for tanweer@test.com; currently viewing page 2 of /orders.

steps.action: Log out via [data-test=nav-sign-out], then log back in with tanweer@test.com / tanweer123, navigate to /orders, go to page 2.
steps.expectedResult: Same total (23) and same "Showing 11–20 of 23", same 10 order rows/ids as before logout (AC8).

tests.note: Question for BA / observed-behavior flag: orders are currently persisted under a single global `orders` localStorage key (context/ShopContext.tsx), not scoped per user account. This AC will pass trivially today even under a different login, since there is no per-user isolation yet. Not a PRD-silence ambiguity — the PRD assumes per-customer history — but worth surfacing before pagination is built on top of the current persistence model.

tests.summary: Orders: exactly 20 orders shows two full pages with Next hidden on page 2
tests.precondition: Exactly 20 orders exist (2 full pages of 10), currently on page 2 of /orders.

steps.action: Read [data-test=order-row] count and [data-test=order-count] (PROPOSED — not yet implemented).
steps.expectedResult: Exactly 10 rows render; count reads "Showing 11–20 of 20" (AC6).
Control is absent or rendered disabled — page 2 is the last page at this exact boundary (AC5).

tests.summary: Orders: no order lost or duplicated across all pages of a 21-order set
tests.precondition: 21 orders exist with distinct known IDs, on /orders.

steps.expectedResult: 21 distinct order IDs total, each appearing exactly once across the full paginated set (AC9).
